gene_description: Lysophospholipase Like 1 — enzyme involved in triglyceride metabolism and fat distribution. Variants at this locus show strong sexual dimorphism, primarily affecting waist-hip ratio and visceral fat distribution in women.

source.title: Meta-analysis identifies 13 new loci associated with waist-hip ratio and reveals sexual dimorphism in the genetic basis of fat distribution
source.finding: LYPLAL1 rs2605100 is associated with waist-hip ratio with marked sexual dimorphism (stronger effect in women). The G allele associates with increased fasting triglycerides, fasting insulin, and central adiposity pattern.

GG.effect: Homozygous G carriers show increased waist-hip ratio, primarily in women. Associated with elevated fasting triglycerides and fasting insulin. Central fat distribution pattern with higher visceral fat tendency. May associate with hepatic steatosis risk. Sexual dimorphism: effect is significantly stronger in females.
GG.recommendation: Focus on waist circumference reduction through exercise (both aerobic and resistance training). Anti-inflammatory diet. For women: regular waist circumference monitoring. Reduce refined carbohydrates to manage triglycerides and insulin.

GA.effect: Heterozygous carriers show modest central adiposity tendency, more pronounced in women. Mildly elevated triglycerides.
GA.recommendation: Standard healthy diet and exercise. Monitor waist circumference.

AG.effect: Heterozygous carriers show modest central adiposity tendency, more pronounced in women. Mildly elevated triglycerides.
AG.recommendation: Standard healthy diet and exercise. Monitor waist circumference.

AA.effect: Wild-type with standard fat distribution pattern. Lower central adiposity tendency from this locus.
